back: An open-source storage layer that adds a transaction log to Parquet files, making data-lake storage reliable.
An ordered record of every change to a table. A change counts only once logged, so readers never see half-finished writes.
The reliability standard (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability) that keeps data correct even with concurrent access.
A change either fully happens or doesn't happen at all — never half-applied.
Querying or restoring previous versions of a Delta table, making mistakes recoverable and auditing easy.
Checking incoming data against the table's expected columns and types, and rejecting data that doesn't fit.
Deliberately changing a table's structure over time in a safe, controlled way.
A single operation that updates existing rows and inserts new ones — ideal for keeping a table in sync with a source.
One Delta table can be written by a scheduled batch job and a real-time stream at the same time, reliably.
The popular open columnar file format that Delta Lake builds on and extends with its transaction log.
